title: Combined Metagenomic / Metatranscriptomic Pipeline for Host-associated Microbiomes
author: Scott Daniel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d7m9k5

steps.title: Get sample data from github (requires...
steps.instruction: Get sample data from github (requires git to be installed on your system). git clone https://github.com/hurwitzlab/mg-sample-data.git This can be downloaded to your shared supercomputers (job scheduler managed) or your own laptop. It's small enough data (< 10mb) that it should be trivial to run on most systems.

steps.title: Get recipe for singularity image containing...
steps.instruction: Get recipe for singularity image containing fastqc and solexaqa: (This must be downloaded to a system where you have sudo privileges) git clone https://github.com/hurwitzlab/singularity-fastqc.git

steps.title: From within /singularity-fastqc run
steps.instruction: From within /singularity-fastqc run ./create_and_bootstrap.sh (see example). This should create a 'fastqc.img'. cd singularity-fastqc/ && ./create_and_bootstrap.sh Make sure there are no errors! E.g. "ABORT: Aborting with RETVAL=255" http://null

steps.title: sftp or scp the created "fastqc
steps.instruction: sftp or scp the created "fastqc.img" to your shared supercomputer directory OR put it in your "/bin" directory (or other dir in your PATH) Example:

steps.title: Generate quality reports with fastqc (optional)
steps.instruction: Generate quality reports with fastqc (optional). Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/00-fastqc-reports.shThis script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job scheduler by issuing the command Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/00-fastqc-reports.sh This script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job scheduler by issuing the command "./00-fastqc-reports.sh" but can be edited to run with other job schedulers (must also edit ./workers/fastqc.sh). If you want to manually generate fastqc reports, the script you want is in 'scripts/workers/fastqc.sh' This is true for most other steps.

steps.title: Run trim_galore (a wrapper script for...
steps.instruction: Run trim_galore (a wrapper script for cutadapt) to cut off low-quality bases and adapters (default is to cut off bases until the probability that the base call was correct is 99% or greater) Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/01-trim-galore.shThis script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job array scheduler by running the command Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/01-trim-galore.sh This script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job array scheduler by running the command "./01-trim-galore.sh" but can be edited to run with other job schedulers (must also edit "./workers/trimgalore.sh")

steps.title: Compare quality reports between steps 5 and 6
steps.instruction: Compare quality reports between steps 5 and 6. If you don't see much improvement, consider re-running step 6 with different parameters.

steps.title: Get the github repos for building...
steps.instruction: Get the github repos for building the images git clone https://github.com/hurwitzlab/singularity-tuxedo.gitgit clone https://github.com/hurwitzlab/singularity-taxoner.git git clone https://github.com/hurwitzlab/singularity-tuxedo.git git clone https://github.com/hurwitzlab/singularity-taxoner.git

steps.title: From within /singularity-tuxedo (Tuxedo is a...
steps.instruction: From within /singularity-tuxedo (Tuxedo is a suite of tools that contains bowtie and cuffdiff see http://software.broadinstitute.org/cancer/software/genepattern/rna-seq-analysis#tuxedo) run ./create_and_bootstrap.sh (see example). This should create a 'bowcuff.img'. From within /singularity-taxoner run ./create_and_bootstrap.sh. This should create a 'taxoner.img' Make sure there are no errors! E.g. "ABORT: Aborting with RETVAL=255" http://null cd singularity-tuxedo/ && ./create_and_bootstrap.sh

steps.title: sftp or scp the created 'bowcuff
steps.instruction: sftp or scp the created 'bowcuff.img' and 'taxoner.img' to your shared supercomputer directory OR put it in your '/bin' directory (or other dir in your PATH) Example:

steps.title: Run 02-taxadb-prep
steps.instruction: Run 02-taxadb-prep.pbs to prepare the lineage file and genome fasta's for bowtie2 indexing Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/02-taxadb-prep.pbsThis script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job scheduler by issuing the command Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/02-taxadb-prep.pbs This script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job scheduler by issuing the command "qsub 02-taxadb-prep.pbs" but can be edited to run with other job schedulers.

steps.title: Make Bowtie2 indices of the split multi-genome fasta files
steps.instruction: Make Bowtie2 indices of the split multi-genome fasta files. The example script will launch bowtie2 to run index building on two computers in parallel. This is why you split the giant fasta into files of ~4 Gb each. So if you had 40 gb worth of genomes in one big file, you could have 10 computers doing the bowtie2 indexing at once. Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/03-bowtie2-build.shThis script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job scheduler by issuing the command Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/03-bowtie2-build.sh This script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job scheduler by issuing the command "./03-bowtie2-build.sh" but can be edited to run with other job schedulers (must also edit ./workers/bowtie2-build.sh).

steps.title: Edit config
steps.instruction: Edit config.sh with your favorite text editor to set variables (directories mainly) Example:

steps.title: Get the singularity container for centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Get the singularity container for centrifuge a classifier program for bacterial/viral species (https://ccb.jhu.edu/software/centrifuge/) If doing this step for the species identification you can optionally skip QC and definitely skip Bowtie2 Prep git clone https://github.com/scottdaniel/singularity-centrifuge.git

steps.title: Build the singularity container as before...
steps.instruction: Build the singularity container as before cd singularity-centrifuge && make img

steps.title: We need to build a couple...
steps.instruction: We need to build a couple more singularity images for this section: singularity-taxoner and singularity-tuxedo (named after the tuxedo suite of tools: bowtie2, cufflinks, etc.)

steps.title: Run taxoner for the DNA reads...
steps.instruction: Run taxoner for the DNA reads to determine species composition Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/04-taxoner.shThis script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job scheduler by issuing the command Example script is mg-sample-data/scripts/04-taxoner.sh This script is meant to be submitted to a PBS job scheduler by issuing the command "./04-taxoner.sh" but can be edited to run with other job schedulers (must also edit ./workers/runTaxoner.sh).
